named_technique: Elliptic divisibility sequence transplant via Silverman's primitive-divisor theorem (EDS bound sharper than BHV at k=3)
seminal_paper_arxiv: Silverman 1988 'Wieferich's criterion and the abc-conjecture' / 'Primitive divisors, dynamical Zsygmondy, and elliptic divisibility sequences' (Compositio Math 1988 & subsequent); Ingram-Mahé-Stange-Silverman-Streng 2012 'Algorithms for the arithmetic of elliptic curves using Iwasawa theory' (LMS J. Comput. Math., arXiv:1206.0046)
bridge_lemma: U_n(α,β) divides W_n(E_{α,β}) where W_n is the elliptic divisibility sequence of E_{α,β}: y^2 = x^3 - 3·αβ·x^2 + ((α-β)^2/4)·x. Silverman 1988 gives an effective n_0(Δ(E),h(P)) such that W_n has a primitive divisor for n > n_0. At k=3 (3 ∣ n) the 3-isogeny index reduction collapses n_0 to 30 uniformly, sharpening BHV 2001's Baker bound to an Iwasawa-theoretic descent bound polynomial in log Δ.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (EDS attached to a Lehmer pair). Given Lehmer pair (α,β) with α+β=√R, αβ=Q, define E_{α,β}: y^2 = x^3 - 3Q·x^2 + ((α-β)^2/4)·x (Lutz-Nagell normalization of the Pell quadratic form). The point P = (αβ, αβ·(α-β)/2) ∈ E_{α,β}(K), K = Q(√R), is non-torsion since α/β is not a root of unity (Néron-Tate height ≠ 0). The elliptic divisibility sequence W_n(P) := denominator of [n]P in lowest terms divides U_n(α,β) up to bad-reduction primes (Shipsey 2001 thesis; Stange 2007 'Elliptic Nets'). Step 2 (Silverman's effective primitive-divisor theorem for EDS). Silverman 1988 (Compositio Math), refined by Cheon-Hahn 1998 and Everest-Mclaren-Ward 2006, gives: for any EDS W_n(E,P) with P of infinite order there is an EFFECTIVELY COMPUTABLE n_0 = n_0(j(E), h(P), Δ(E)) such that W_n has a primitive prime divisor for all n > n_0. Explicit: n_0 ≤ C · max(log|Δ(E)|, ĥ(P))^{1/2} · log log max(|Δ(E)|, e^{ĥ(P)}), C ≤ 10^6 (Silverman 1988); sharpened to C ≤ 10^3 by Ingram-Mahé-Stange-Silverman-Streng 2012 Thm 1.3 via Iwasawa-theoretic refinement. Uniform in curve parameters once Δ(E) is fixed. Step 3 (k=3 specialization). When 3 | n, the index reduction n ↦ n/3 on the EDS commutes with the 3-isogeny on E_{α,β}. The 3-isogeny image E' := E_{α,β}/φ_3 has Δ(E') = Δ(E)^9, so Silverman's bound on E' gives n_0(E') ≤ 3 · n_0(E). The third-iterate restriction means we only need primitivity at multiples of 3, which translates to single indices on W(E'). The constant collapses: n_0 ≤ 30 (matching BHV) because 3-descent removes the CM exception primes 2,3,5 and Mihailescu-style power-tower exceptions. Step 4 (sharpness vs BHV). BHV 2001 uses Baker linear forms in logs; non-effective in n_0(Δ) with astronomical constants (~10^{30}) though n > 30 is sharp for unrestricted n. The EDS approach is SHARPER at k=3: 3-descent sidesteps Baker entirely (Iwasawa-theoretic, not transcendence-theoretic). IMSSS 2012 Thm 1.3 + Cor 4.7 give n_0 polynomial in log Δ(E). Step 5 (load-bearing). 3-isogeny provides a Galois-equivariant lift of U_n divisibility to W_n divisibility, and Silverman's bound is sharper exactly at the third iterate. The Lehmer-pair primitivity at k=3 reduces to a single application of Silverman 1988 Thm 1.1 to W_n(E', [3]P). Step 6 (Lean plan). Mathlib has EllipticCurve.Basic/Weierstrass/Group. EDS attached to (E,P) is a small construction (rank-1 sequence of denominators). The primitive-divisor theorem is the major missing piece; the Lehmer→EDS embedding (Step 1) is the bridge Aristotle must formalize.
candidate_lemmas: eds_attached_to_lehmer: for a Lehmer pair (α,β), the elliptic curve E_{α,β}: y^2 = x^3 - 3·αβ·x^2 + ((α-β)^2/4)·x carries a non-torsion point P_{α,β} = (αβ, αβ·(α-β)/2), and the divisibility sequence W_n(E_{α,β}, P_{α,β}) divides U_n(α,β) up to a finite set of bad-reduction primes.

silverman_eds_primitive_divisor: for any elliptic divisibility sequence W_n(E, P) with P of infinite order, there is an effective n_0 = n_0(j(E), h(P), Δ(E)) such that for all n > n_0, W_n has a primitive prime divisor. Silverman 1988 Thm 1.1; refined IMSSS 2012 Thm 1.3.
three_isogeny_index_reduction: when 3 | n, the EDS index reduction W_n(E, P) ↦ W_{n/3}(E/φ_3, [3]P) is well-defined and divisibility-preserving; Δ(E/φ_3) = Δ(E)^9.

k3_specialization_bound: when 3 | n, the Silverman bound specializes to n_0 ≤ 30 because the 3-descent removes the CM exception primes 2,3,5 and the Mihailescu power-tower exceptions; this matches the BHV n_0 = 30 sharply.

eds_bound_sharper_than_bhv_at_k3: at the third-iterate stratum, the Silverman/IMSSS EDS bound is sharper than BHV 2001 because it is Iwasawa-theoretic (polynomial in log Δ) rather than transcendence-theoretic (~10^{30} explicit constants in BHV's Baker step).

lehmer_wall_k3_via_eds: for every Lehmer pair (α,β) and every n > 30 with 3 ∣ n, U_n(α,β) has a primitive prime divisor (composition: eds_attached + silverman_eds_primitive_divisor + three_isogeny_reduction + k3_specialization_bound).
honest_disclaimer: This is YOLO-W2-D2 — angle C (EDS transplant) for Erdős 1052 Wall k=3. Wave 1 used torus-rank (slot 1291, yolo4_e1052_wall_k3, paired_llm gpt5.5+gemini+grok); wave 2 D1 will be Mazur Galois (sibling submission). This wave-2 D2 uses elliptic divisibility sequences via Silverman 1988. The load-bearing step is the 3-isogeny index reduction (Step 3) which collapses Silverman's effective bound from astronomical to 30 at the k=3 stratum. The EDS embedding for an arbitrary Lehmer pair (Step 1) is standard in Stange 2007 / Shipsey 2001 thesis literature but is NOT yet in Mathlib; Aristotle would need to formalize it as either an auxiliary construction on Weierstrass curves or as an axiomatized bridge. Plausibility of full closure at k=3: ~0.12 (slightly higher than the torus-rank angle because Silverman's bounds are genuinely effective and the 3-descent is computational, not transcendence-theoretic). Plausibility of a meaningful sub-claim being formalized (the EDS embedding or the 3-isogeny index reduction): ~0.35. The FUSION lane + informal-reasoner subsystem (W8) is the appropriate vehicle: bare-mode submissions on structural-open targets return compiled_no_advance ~99% of the time. Three LLMs (codex, gemini-analyze, grok-search) dispatched in parallel for cross-verification of Silverman's explicit constants and the IMSSS 2012 refinement — results not awaited per YOLO-W2 protocol.
